content.method: Simulation/microsimulation using existing enrollment and residential data to model plausible displacement scenarios, supplemented by qualitative interviews with school maintainers and parents on likely substitution behavior; where possible, exploit natural variation from past administrative desegregation episodes in Hungary as a quasi-experiment.
content.question: If the 6/8-year tracks were abolished or delayed without complementary reforms to school choice, maintainer exemptions, and teacher allocation, how much sorting behavior would displace into private/church schools, cross-municipal transfers, or tutoring-based selection rather than disappear?
content.why_it_matters: This directly determines whether T2 or T6 alone can succeed, or whether they must be bundled with T4-style zoning/choice reform and T3-style admissions reform to avoid merely relocating segregation, as occurred in Finland and Sweden post-1990s.
